title: delivery.rs: 429 Too Many Requests triggers circuit breaker instead of respecting Retry-After
description:
LOCATION: src/delivery.rs:294-349, process_batch()

FINDING: The success arm only matches 'resp.status().is_success()' (2xx) and the 410 arm. HTTP 202 Accepted is a 2xx status and IS caught by is_success(), so it is handled correctly. However, Mastodon and other AP servers commonly return 202 Accepted, which is within 2xx. This part is fine.

The actual bug is subtler: when an inbox returns any non-2xx, non-410 status (e.g. 429 Too Many Requests, 503, 401), the code calls handle_retry() AND records a circuit-breaker failure. A 429 or 503 with a Retry-After header is treated identically to a permanent 5xx failure — there is no backoff based on the Retry-After header, and repeated 429s count toward the circuit breaker trip threshold of 10. This causes legitimate rate-limited remotes to be circuit-broken after 10 retries rather than respected.

IMPACT: Delivery to busy/rate-limiting servers gets permanently circuit-broken rather than respecting Retry-After, leading to missed deliveries to large instances that rate-limit aggressively.
close_reason: Fixed: 429 responses handled separately, no longer trip circuit breaker
